# Management Is Part of Role Design

**Flexibility: Expected**

## The belief

Every role is designed *with* its **management and collaboration layer** —
reporting line, peers, communication topology, delegation, and escalation — not
as an afterthought once the role is already running. And **adversarial review is
one tool within management, not the whole of oversight.** A role that is well
managed is coached, paired, handed off to, and escalated through — the full
repertoire a good manager uses, not a single check.

## Why we believe this

We have historically over-indexed on review/audit as if it were the entirety of
oversight; the founding thesis (JOS-C53) is explicit that adversarial review is
just one small piece of how an organization manages its people. Real management
is a repertoire: setting context, defining handoffs, balancing peer tension
(QA vs. continuous-improvement), delegating, and escalating. If management is
designed in from the start — who this role reports to, who its peers are, how
work flows to and from it — the role slots cleanly into the org chart and its
oversight is coherent. Bolting management on later produces orphan roles with no
manager (which JOS-P04 already forbids) and oversight that is all-stick,
no-coaching.

## What this looks like in practice

- Role design specifies its org placement: manager, peers, who it delegates to,
  comms channels, and escalation path — as first-class fields, not prose.
- The management repertoire is explicit: handoffs, pairing/peer-review,
  coaching, and escalation — with adversarial review as one named tool among them.
- A manager seat reconciles the tension between complementary roles (e.g., a
  Guardian and an Improver) rather than letting "review" stand in for management.

## What this rules out

- Designing a role's capabilities while leaving its management/collaboration
  unspecified.
- Treating adversarial review as the complete oversight story.
- Orphan roles with no manager, peers, or escalation path.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C53]** Agents in an Org Chart (founds this Principle).
- **[JOS-P04]** Mandatory Escalation (every role has a manager + escalation).
- **[JOS-P01]** Trust & Graduated Autonomy (management moves authority on evidence).
- **[JOS-P27]** Agents and Humans Are the Same Kind of Role (managed the same way).
